description: Unit A — block-api write ergonomics (FGAP-083 + FGAP-084 + FGAP-082). One root cause: the items schema can be a $ref (#/definitions/<kind>) and file-block-item/append-relation never dereference it. Add canonical $ref-aware block-api helpers (resolveBlockItemSchema + nextId), repoint the CLI auto-id + auto-stamp + dry-run to resolved-schema + whole-file validation, add the in-pi append-block-item autoId twin (DEC-0019/0020). block-api write path already validates whole-file $ref-aware; only orchestrator-side pre-validation + auto-id were broken (hit filing FEAT-004).
acceptance_criteria: block-api: resolveBlockItemSchema (finds arrayKey + derefs #/definitions|#/$defs items $ref; inline passthrough; throw on unresolvable) + nextId(cwd,block) ($ref-aware pattern read, prefix+width parse, scan-max, pad)

file-block-item: loadBlockSchema uses resolved item schema (required/properties/id.pattern work for $ref blocks -> created_by/created_at auto-stamp restored); autoAllocateId -> nextId; dry-run -> whole-file validateFromFile (dry-run PASS == write PASS)
append-relation: dry-run validates prospective whole array, not the items fragment
index.ts: append-block-item gains optional autoId param -> nextId when id absent (Pi-tool twin of --auto-id)
block-api.test.ts: resolveBlockItemSchema (inline + $ref + throw) + nextId (inline/$ref blocks, 3/4/variable width, empty=>first, scan-max, no-pattern throw) + $ref-block whole-file append validation
Runtime demo: nextId features=>FEAT-005, framework-gaps=>FGAP-096, decisions=>DEC-0045; file-block-item --block features --auto-id --dry-run on a feature-without-id allocates + auto-stamps + PASS
Fresh-context adversarial: deref both shapes, widths, whole-file $ref parity + dry-run==write, in-pi autoId, no inline-block regression, no remaining fragment-validation in CLIs
build/check/test green; skills regenerated (append-block-item desc changed)
notes: Unit A of the A/B/C operational-friction cluster. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/idempotent-dancing-wilkes.md. Closes FGAP-083/084/082.
